title: Mercor: Redefining the Labor Market with AI
content:
If you were a job seeker on professional networking sites recently, you might have noticed a surge in unconventional job listings. Companies like Mercor have flooded platforms with offers paying hundreds of dollars an hour for specialized expertise. Mercor's founders argue that artificial intelligence will not destroy work, but rather push people into different kinds of employment. They compare this shift to the agricultural revolution, noting that technological advancement changes the nature of work instead of eliminating it. Ultimately, they believe AI will handle repetitive tasks, creating more value, opportunity, and societal progress.

To realize this vision, these companies are building AI-powered tools that act as global labor aggregators. Algorithms aim to perfectly match billions of workers with millions of companies, treating traditional full-time employment as a relic. Instead of hiring conventional employees, companies can purchase discrete units of expertise completed by the best-suited individuals worldwide. This model began with screening software engineers but quickly pivoted when AI developers demanded domain experts to train their models. Today, these platforms stand at the forefront of redefining how human expertise is distributed in the modern labor market.
